tr.td: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Human isophane insulin 70 unit/mL and recombinant human regular insulin 30 unit/mL solution for injection
10 units (70/30 insulin) subcutaneously before breakfast and dinner
1 tablet (25 mg) by mouth twice daily
1 tablet (25 mg) by mouth once daily
1 tablet (40 mg) by mouth once daily
1 tablet (10 mg) by mouth once daily
Albuterol (as albuterol sulfate) 830 microgram/mL solution for inhalation
2.5 mg (3 mL of 830 microgram/mL solution) via nebulizer every 4 to 6 hours as needed
Alendronic acid (as alendronate sodium) 10 mg oral tablet
1 tablet (50 mg) by mouth once daily
Clopidogrel (as clopidogrel bisulfate) 75 mg oral tablet
1 tablet (75 mg) by mouth once daily
1 tablet (20 mg) by mouth once daily
400 micrograms sublingually as needed for chest pain, may repeat every 5 minutes as needed, up to 3 doses in 15 minutes
Metoprolol tartrate (as metoprolol succinate) 100 mg prolonged-release oral tablet
Influenza virus antigen only vaccine product
Clostridium tetani toxoid antigen adsorbed only vaccine product
Assessment of health and social care needs
Depression screening using Patient Health Questionnaire Two-Item score (procedure)
Assessment using AUDIT-C (Alcohol Use Disorders Identification Test - Consumption)
Depression screening using PHQ-9 (Patient Health Questionnaire 9) score
Inadequate digital access affecting health care

ul.li: Maintain daily weight within 2 kg of baseline to detect early fluid retention.
Maintain systolic blood pressure below 130 mmHg and diastolic below 80 mmHg.
Limit sodium intake to less than 2 grams per day.
Restrict total daily fluid intake to less than 1.5 liters per day.
Patient will recognize and promptly report symptoms of heart failure exacerbation such as increased shortness of breath, swelling, or rapid weight gain.
Patient will demonstrate understanding of prediabetes and implement recommended lifestyle changes to reduce risk of progression to diabetes.
Maintain hemoglobin A1c at or below 6.4% to prevent progression to diabetes.
Achieve at least 5% reduction in body weight through diet and exercise.
Maintain blood pressure below 130/80 mmHg to reduce cardiovascular risk.
Engage in at least 150 minutes of moderate-intensity physical activity per week.
Achieve and maintain blood pressure within recommended target range through lifestyle modifications.
Reduce daily sodium intake to less than 2.3 grams per day to support blood pressure control.
Achieve and maintain a healthy body mass index (BMI) through diet and physical activity.
Engage in at least 150 minutes of moderate-intensity aerobic physical activity per week as tolerated.
Patient will demonstrate correct inhaler technique and adhere to prescribed asthma action plan to minimize risk of asthma exacerbations.
Patient will maintain well-controlled asthma symptoms with minimal use of rescue inhaler.
Patient will use rescue inhaler no more than 2 times per week.

tr.td: Vaccine product containing only Influenza virus antigen (medicinal product)
Pain severity - 0-10 verbal numeric rating [Score] - Reported
Urea nitrogen [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma
Carbon dioxide, total [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma
Glucose [Mass/volume] in Urine by Test strip
Glucose [Presence] in Urine by Test strip
Bilirubin.total [Mass/volume] in Urine by Test strip
Bilirubin.total [Presence] in Urine by Test strip
Ketones [Mass/volume] in Urine by Test strip
Ketones [Presence] in Urine by Test strip
Specific gravity of Urine by Test strip
pH of Urine by Test strip
Protein [Mass/volume] in Urine by Test strip
Protein [Presence] in Urine by Test strip
Nitrite [Presence] in Urine by Test strip
Hemoglobin [Presence] in Urine by Test strip
Leukocyte esterase [Presence] in Urine by Test strip

ul.li: Maintain daily weight within 2 kg of baseline to detect early fluid retention.
Maintain systolic blood pressure below 130 mmHg and diastolic below 80 mmHg.
Limit sodium intake to less than 2 grams per day.
Restrict total daily fluid intake to less than 1.5 liters per day.
Patient will recognize and promptly report symptoms of heart failure exacerbation such as increased shortness of breath, swelling, or rapid weight gain.

display.value: Heart failure self management plan (record artifact)

ul.li: Patient will demonstrate understanding of prediabetes and implement recommended lifestyle changes to reduce risk of progression to diabetes.
Maintain hemoglobin A1c at or below 6.4% to prevent progression to diabetes.
Achieve at least 5% reduction in body weight through diet and exercise.
Maintain blood pressure below 130/80 mmHg to reduce cardiovascular risk.
Engage in at least 150 minutes of moderate-intensity physical activity per week.
Achieve and maintain blood pressure within recommended target range through lifestyle modifications.
Reduce daily sodium intake to less than 2.3 grams per day to support blood pressure control.
Achieve and maintain a healthy body mass index (BMI) through diet and physical activity.
Engage in at least 150 minutes of moderate-intensity aerobic physical activity per week as tolerated.
Patient will demonstrate correct inhaler technique and adhere to prescribed asthma action plan to minimize risk of asthma exacerbations.
Patient will maintain well-controlled asthma symptoms with minimal use of rescue inhaler.
Patient will use rescue inhaler no more than 2 times per week.
